macro_segments.title: Introduction and Overview of Marriage Support Obligations

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and daf location
Review of previous sugya on marriage troubles and get proceedings
Introduction to financial penalties for mored and moredes
Dinar, zuz, and sela equivalencies; mohar calculation
Mathematical calculation of weeks to deplete ketubah at 7 zuz per week

macro_segments.title: Tarpeikin Currency and Meal Value Definitions

micro_segments.title: What is tarpeikin; astira and its equivalence to half a zuz
Rabbi Yehudah's opinion on three tarpeikin equals nine ma'in
Comparison of penalties: 7 zuz vs 7 tarpeikin per week

macro_segments.title: Differential Penalties for Mored and Moredes: Rationales and Distinctions

micro_segments.title: Why husband fined half per day, wife fined full per day
Why wife fined on Shabbat but husband not: appearance of payment for labor
Marketplace of prostitutes: men hire women, determining sexual desire disparity
Physical/biological argument: external vs internal genitalia and sexual arousal visibility
Introduction to hamshrah and minimum food allowance outside shared domicile
Grain allowance: two kav wheat or four kav barley per week
Rabbi Yosi on barley limited to Rabbi Yishmael near Edom
Legumes, oil, dried fruits or pressed dates per week
Durable goods: bed, mat, cushion, head covering, belt, shoes
Clothing: 50 zuz yearly; seasonal purchase timing to avoid waste
Pin money (ma'ah kesef) weekly and requirement to eat Friday night meal together
Ma'aseh yadayim: wife's work obligation and distinction from earning potential
Weaving amounts: five sla'im of warp or ten of woof per week in Judah
Reduction of work and increase of food if wife is nursing
Application to poor persons; adjustment for wealthy status according to honor

macro_segments.title: Tannaitic Dispute on Meal Measurements and Bread Quantities

micro_segments.title: Mishnah sources for eruv techumin contribution as two meals per person
Rabbi Meir vs Rabbi Yehudah on weekday vs Shabbat meal amounts
Rabbi Yochanan ben Beroka: half a kav wheat equals two meals
Rabbi Shimon: two-thirds loaf when three loaves per kav
Kedei achilat pras as standard eating time measurement

macro_segments.title: Calculation of Two Kav Grain into Weekly Meal Count

micro_segments.title: Eight meals according to Rabbi Yochanan ben Beroka; eighteen according to Rabbi Shimon
Problem: neither eight nor eighteen equals fourteen weekly meals
Rav Chisda: baker's profit of one-third must be factored into grain calculation
With baker's profit adjusted, twelve meals; with Shabbat guest allowance reaches appropriate total
Rabbi Chidka opinion requiring four meals on Shabbat; three meals requires guest allowance

macro_segments.title: Analysis of Missing Food Items and Nutritional Content

micro_segments.title: Absence of protein, meat, fish, eggs, cheese, milk and vegetables from minimum allowance
Rabbi Elazar: no wine allocated to wife when living separately from husband
Hosea 2:7 derashah suggesting women should not drink wine without husband
Braita exception: accustomed women get one cup; unaccustomed get none
Abaye's clarification: accustomed with husband gets two, without gets one; unaccustomed with husband gets one, without gets none
Alternative: wine for cooking allowed even when drinking prohibited
Story of Nakdimon ben Guryon's daughter-in-law allocated wine for cooking

macro_segments.title: Tannaitic Teaching on Wine Consumption and Female Licentiousness

micro_segments.title: One cup good for woman; two unseemly; three leads to verbal sexual solicitation
Four cups: woman will solicit animals in public without shame
Rava clarification: restrictions apply only when husband absent; no restriction when husband present
Exception for guests: marital relations restricted in another's home even with spouse present

macro_segments.title: Stories of Women Seeking Food Allocation and Wine

micro_segments.title: Homa (Abaye's wife) seeks food allocation from Rava; then requests wine
Rava claims Abaye never drank wine; Homa reveals his private wine drinking
Homa's arm exposed; Rava becomes excited and goes home to wife
Rava's wife pursues Homa from Mechuza with strap; accusations of killing three husbands
Story of Rav Yosef's wife seeking allocation; Rav Nechemia allocates wine based on Mechuza custom
